batch_note: Phase A — chains by total review count. Chains covered: My Aged Care (4 branches, 319 chain reviews), Mintygreen (6 branches, 302 chain reviews), Amazing Grace (2 branches, 296 chain reviews). Operator sites myagedcare.com.my and mintygreen-wellness.com were blocked by network proxy; services and pricing sourced from repo research files (pricing-benchmarks.md) and GROUPS descriptions in data.js which document operator-published data. Amazing Grace has no public website — conservative stubs written.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care Sdn Bhd is the licensed operating company behind a cluster of nursing home branches concentrated in Petaling Jaya, Selangor. The company was established in 2008 by a team of medical professionals who collectively brought over six decades of healthcare experience. From a single original property, My Aged Care has grown to five Petaling Jaya locations — the flagship My Manor in PJS 7, a branch on Jalan Othman in PJ Old Town, one on Jalan Assunta immediately adjacent to Assunta Hospital, a Jalan University site — plus a sixth location in Ipoh. Every branch is licensed as a nursing home, meaning the standard of care exceeds that of a basic residential welfare home.

Across its branches, My Aged Care provides nursing care for bedridden and high-dependency residents, post-surgical recovery, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia management — all under 24-hour nursing with hourly resident rounds. The group's published packages, as listed on its website, start from RM 2,500 per month for basic residential care and rise to RM 4,500 for higher-dependency needs; a dedicated post-surgical recovery package is listed from RM 6,300 per month. Day care is available at RM 10–15 per hour. An in-house physiotherapy service operates under the MAC Physio Corner name at select branches.

For families in greater Petaling Jaya, the choice between My Aged Care branches typically comes down to location and care dependency. The Jalan Assunta branch carries a clinical edge for residents with frequent hospital needs; My Manor in PJS 7 offers a larger, lower-density setting than the converted-shophouse format. The PJ Old Town and Jalan University branches suit families on the northern PJ side. Ask which specific branches currently run MAC Physio Corner, confirm current room availability, and request written pricing matched to your resident's care level.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ My Manor is the founding branch and flagship property of My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, located along Jalan Taman 7/6 in PJS 7, Petaling Jaya — set back from the main road behind the MBPJ City Council building. The property spans roughly 10,000 square feet and was deliberately designed at a lower resident density than converted-shophouse nursing homes, providing more shared space per resident. My Aged Care was established in 2008 by medical professionals, and My Manor has been the group's anchor location since inception. It holds a 4.5-star rating from 142 Google reviews, one of the larger review pools for a single nursing home property in Petaling Jaya.

Across its branches, My Aged Care provides nursing care for bedridden and high-dependency residents, post-surgical recovery, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia management — all under 24-hour nursing with hourly resident rounds. The group's published packages start from RM 2,500 per month for basic residential care and rise to RM 4,500 for higher-dependency needs; a post-surgical recovery package is listed from RM 6,300 per month. Day care runs at RM 10–15 per hour. An in-house physiotherapy service operates under the MAC Physio Corner name at select branches.

At 142 Google reviews, My Manor has one of the stronger review trails among PJ nursing homes, with recurring feedback noting attentive nursing staff and consistent communication with families. The low-density format means the total bed count here is smaller than at converted-shophouse sites — room availability can be tight, so early enquiry is advisable. The PJS 7 location has nearby clinics but is not directly adjacent to a major hospital; for residents requiring frequent specialist access, the group's Jalan Assunta branch sits immediately next to Assunta Hospital.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ PJ Old Town operates along Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus in PJ Old Town — a central Petaling Jaya neighbourhood with established amenities, good transport links, and Assunta Hospital roughly 1.5 km away. It is one of five Petaling Jaya branches operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd since 2008, a company founded by medical professionals with a combined six decades of healthcare experience. This branch carries a 4.8-star rating from 92 Google reviews — the highest rating in the My Aged Care network on a meaningful review volume, reflecting a well-regarded track record with resident families.

Across its branches, My Aged Care provides nursing care for bedridden and high-dependency residents, post-surgical recovery, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia management — all under 24-hour nursing with hourly resident rounds. Published packages start from RM 2,500 per month for basic residential care and reach RM 4,500 for higher-dependency needs; a post-surgical recovery package starts from RM 6,300 per month. Day care is available at RM 10–15 per hour. An in-house physiotherapy service under the MAC Physio Corner name operates at select branches.

The 4.8-star average from 92 reviewers is the strongest single-branch rating in the My Aged Care group, with recurring feedback pointing to consistent nursing quality and responsive communication with families. The Jalan Othman–Jalan Terus premises are in a converted-shophouse format — typical of inner-PJ nursing homes — with a higher resident density than the My Manor flagship in PJS 7. Families considering this branch should enquire early about room availability, as PJ Old Town draws high demand for nursing home placements. For residents needing very close hospital access, the group's Jalan Assunta branch is immediately adjacent to Assunta Hospital.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ Jalan Assunta sits at 9 Jalan Assunta 4/52 in Petaling Jaya, placed directly adjacent to Assunta Hospital — one of the most clinically strategic addresses a nursing home can occupy in the PJ area. When a resident requires emergency care, specialist follow-up, or post-discharge step-down nursing, proximity to a full-service private hospital is a material practical advantage. The facility is operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, founded in 2008, and carries a 4.4-star rating from 85 Google reviews.

Across its branches, My Aged Care provides nursing care for bedridden and high-dependency residents, post-surgical recovery, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia management — all under 24-hour nursing with hourly resident rounds. Published packages start from RM 2,500 per month for basic residential care and rise to RM 4,500 for higher-dependency residents; a post-surgical recovery package is listed from RM 6,300 per month. Day care runs at RM 10–15 per hour. An in-house physiotherapy service operates under the MAC Physio Corner name at select branches.

The adjacency to Assunta Hospital is the standout feature of this branch — for residents with ongoing specialist appointments, post-surgical step-down needs, or conditions requiring fast hospital access, this location removes a significant logistical burden from families. The 4.4-star rating from 85 reviews is solid on a meaningful review volume. Google Maps categorises this property as a retirement home, though My Aged Care operates it under the same nursing home care model as its other PJ branches. Confirm room availability when you enquire, as small PJ nursing homes fill quickly, and ask about MAC Physio Corner availability at this specific branch.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home(Sungai Long & Kajang ElderCare Center) 加影碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home's Sungai Long–Kajang branch, marketed as the Kajang ElderCare Center (加影碧绿疗养院), is one of the anchor sites in the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Klang Valley network. It is located at Landmark Residences 2 along Jalan Sungai Long in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, Selangor — a residential corridor roughly 25 km southeast of Kuala Lumpur. Mintygreen was founded in 2017, growing from a bungalow start in Desa Permai to a multi-branch chain. The group describes itself as the first senior care operator in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its standard care programme. This listing is the most-reviewed in the Mintygreen network at 4.6 stars from 108 Google reviews.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. The integration of TCM — including acupuncture and Tui Na — is a structural part of the programme rather than an optional add-on. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term residential care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

The Bandar Sungai Long location draws families from across the southern Klang Valley — Balakong, Seri Kembangan, and Kajang town are all within a reasonable drive. At 108 reviews averaging 4.6 stars, this is among the better-documented nursing homes in the Kajang corridor. Families should ask specifically about the TCM session schedule, the clinical capabilities actively available at this branch, and current bed availability. The branch shares a group enquiry line with other Mintygreen locations — when calling, specify the Sungai Long or Kajang ElderCare branch.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras(Sungai Long) 蕉赖碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Senior Care Center (蕉赖碧绿疗养院) is one of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Kajang-area listings, located at Landmark Residences 2 along Jalan Sungai Long in Kajang, Selangor. Founded in 2017, Mintygreen has grown from a single bungalow in Desa Permai to multiple branches across the Klang Valley and Penang, and describes itself as the first senior care operator in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its care model. This Google Maps listing carries a 4.7-star rating from 13 reviews. The Sungai Long address encompasses several Mintygreen listings — families should contact the group directly to clarify which specific unit and beds are available under this entry point.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. TCM — including acupuncture and Tui Na — is built into the standard programme as a structural feature rather than an add-on. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

Families researching Mintygreen's Kajang or Sungai Long options may encounter multiple Google Maps listings for the same area, reflecting the group's multi-floor, multi-shophouse cluster at Landmark Residences 2. When enquiring, specify the care type you need and ask the group to confirm which listing corresponds to the available beds at that time. Ask about the TCM session schedule, which clinical services are actively delivered at this specific unit, and current room availability — then verify details on a site visit.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Kepong and PJ Bandar Sri Damansara)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch occupies commercial space at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9 in Prima Damansara — a development at the northern edge of Petaling Jaya accessible from both Kepong and the Damansara corridor via the Penchala Link and North-South Expressway access roads. Part of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group founded in 2017, this branch serves the northern Klang Valley catchment stretching from Kepong through Sungai Buloh. It holds a 4.1-star rating from 50 Google reviews — the most-reviewed Mintygreen location outside the Sungai Long cluster.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. The chain integrates Traditional Chinese Medicine — acupuncture and Tui Na — into its standard care programme as a structural feature. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

The 4.1-star average from 50 reviews is the lowest in the Mintygreen network; families should read through recent reviews to understand the feedback pattern before visiting. The Sphere Damansara commercial block setting is physically different from the Sungai Long shophouse cluster — the room layout, common areas, and outdoor access here will vary, so a personal site visit is important. When you visit, ask specifically whether the full TCM programme and the clinical services (NG tube, PEG, wound care) are actively staffed at this branch. The Damansara location is well connected to the Kepong and northern PJ road network, which helps for regular family visits.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Suites - Home Care Nursing Cheras KL
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites Cheras is the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Kuala Lumpur property, at 100 Jalan Cheras in Phoenix Business Park, 56000 KL — a few minutes from Taman Connaught MRT and on the Cheras–Kajang Expressway corridor. Unlike the group's shophouse-cluster branches, this outlet operates as a boutique nursing and rehabilitation centre in a converted commercial suite block, offering private hotel-style rooms rather than the shared-floor layout typical elsewhere in the chain. It carries a 4.8-star rating from 22 Google reviews, the second-highest rating in the Mintygreen network.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. TCM — including acupuncture and Tui Na — is integrated into the standard care programme rather than offered as an optional extra. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

The private suite-style room format makes Care Suites Cheras particularly suited to post-surgical recovery or short-to-medium respite stays, where a quieter, more private environment is a priority. The Phoenix Business Park address is served by Taman Connaught MRT, which is useful for family members visiting by public transport. At 22 reviews, the feedback pool is smaller than at the Sungai Long branch; a site visit to view the specific room configuration is important. Ask about the available room types, TCM session scheduling, and which clinical services are fully staffed at this location.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21 is the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Subang Jaya branch, located at 1 Jalan USJ 21/10 in UEP Subang Jaya, Selangor. The USJ area is a mature residential township with good road access and proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC), making it a practical catchment for residents with ongoing specialist care requirements. Mintygreen was founded in 2017 and operates a Klang Valley network of nursing homes distinguished by the integration of Traditional Chinese Medicine into standard care. This USJ21 listing carries a single Google review at 5.0 stars, indicating a newer or less-indexed presence at this location.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. TCM — including acupuncture and Tui Na — is built into the standard programme as a structural feature. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

The proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre is a genuine clinical advantage for residents who need specialist follow-ups or fast hospital access. With a single published Google review, the public feedback is too limited to draw conclusions about care quality at this specific branch — a site visit is strongly recommended before any placement decision. On your visit, ask about the current bed count and room configuration, which clinical services are actively staffed here, and TCM session availability. The Mintygreen group's established track record at its Sungai Long and Cheras branches provides chain context, but verify this branch's scope independently.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center) is the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Sungai Long headquarters, operating from a six-shophouse block at Landmark Residences 2 in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, Selangor. Established in 2017, the group began in a bungalow in Desa Permai before expanding into this purpose-configured multi-unit cluster in Sungai Long. Mintygreen describes itself as the first senior care operator in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its nursing home model. It carries a 4.6-star Google rating from 108 reviews, one of the more substantive review pools among Kajang-area nursing homes.

Across its branches, Mintygreen offers residential nursing care, dementia and memory care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and post-surgical recovery. TCM — including acupuncture and Tui Na — is integrated as a structural feature rather than an optional programme. Clinical capabilities include nasogastric tube management, catheter care, PEG tube feeding, and wound care, with bi-weekly GP visits. Published pricing on the group's website lists long-term care from RM 4,000 per month and day care at RM 220 per day, with final fees depending on care level and room type.

This Bandar Sungai Long site serves the southern Klang Valley catchment — Balakong, Seri Kembangan, and Kajang town are all within a 10–15 minute drive. At 108 reviews averaging 4.6 stars, the public feedback is substantive; families should scan recent reviews for patterns around nursing consistency and TCM delivery. Ask about current bed availability, room type options, and which clinical services are actively maintained at this location. The six-shophouse layout spans several connected units — request a full tour including common areas and outdoor spaces when you visit.

facilities.title: AMAZING GRACE SENIOR HOME
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Senior Home is a residential elder care facility at Jalan SS19/4 in SS19, Subang Jaya — a well-established residential neighbourhood with solid road access from the Subang Jaya and Shah Alam road network. It operates under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, which also runs a second property in Section 14, Petaling Jaya. With a 5.0-star rating from 156 Google reviews, Amazing Grace Senior Home carries one of the highest review counts for a single nursing home property in the Klang Valley, reflecting a well-established and active community of resident families over time.

The SS19 home is categorised by Google Maps as a retirement home, and third-party directories place it in the assisted living tier — suggesting the primary resident population is mobile or semi-mobile elderly rather than fully bedridden, high-dependency cases. Amazing Grace Care Centre does not operate a publicly accessible website, so the full scope of services, bed count, and pricing is not publicly documented. For comparison, the group's Section 14 Petaling Jaya property (Amazing Grace Retirement Home) is listed under nursing home, assisted living, and physiotherapy on Google Maps, suggesting that the PJ branch may cover a broader clinical scope.

The 156 Google reviews at 5.0 stars is an exceptionally strong community signal — at this review volume, a consistent perfect rating reflects sustained performance across a large number of resident families rather than a statistical artefact of a small sample. Families should use the site visit to clarify the care scope: whether the facility provides nursing care for bedridden residents, what the staff-to-resident ratio is, and whether a registered nurse is on-site around the clock. Ask for the JKM licence number on your visit to confirm the regulatory category, and request written pricing information directly from the operator.

facilities.title: AMAZING GRACE RETIREMENT HOME
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Retirement Home is located at 11 Jalan 14/55 in Section 14, Petaling Jaya — a residential neighbourhood with good access to the Sprint Highway and Jalan Utama, and close to the PJ University College campus and the broader Assunta Hospital corridor. This is one of two properties under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand in the PJ–Subang Jaya area. It holds a 4.9-star rating from 140 Google reviews, consistently among the highest review-count profiles for a nursing home in Petaling Jaya.

The Section 14 property is listed on Google Maps under nursing home, assisted living, and physiotherapy — a broader set of care categories than the group's SS19 Subang Jaya sister branch, which is listed only as a retirement home. This suggests the Section 14 facility may cater to a higher-dependency population or a wider clinical scope, including residents in post-stroke or post-surgical physiotherapy programmes. Amazing Grace Care Centre operates no publicly accessible website, so specific services, bed count, and pricing are not publicly documented — request written pricing and a services list directly from the facility.

The 140-review count at 4.9 stars represents one of the most substantive Google profiles for a nursing home in Petaling Jaya, with recurring review themes pointing to attentive staff and consistent family communication. On your site visit, ask about the specific nursing scope: staffing levels, doctor visit frequency, what physiotherapy is available and for which resident types, and whether the facility handles full nursing care for bedridden residents. Ask for the JKM or MOH facility licence details to confirm the regulatory category. For families with high-dependency residents, clarify whether this home can accommodate bedridden patients and NG or PEG tube care.
